text: Description of the image content, meaning that large image portions (beyond the single pixel level) are exposed to deep image analysis tasks. Such image analysis tasks include, but are not limited to, prediction of image appearances of the future or after rotating/translate the current image2. In this work, the following contributions are made: 1. We proposed a method that leverages VQ-GAN for producing 3D high resolution images and at the same time reducing the running time of the method, that is located in the latent space. 2. We evaluated our method using four different public data-sets of various medical image modalities and anatomic locations, with relatively low in number (from n = 1844, down to n = 30). Our implementation and the source code can be downloaded from: https://github.com/seyedali88/SGVI 3. That the generated images from our SGVI-based method significantly outperform those produced from an established state-of-the-art GAN, due to the significantly higher sample diversity and lower mode-caching. 4. Image quality assessment metrics used on synthesized data are less representative of how humans judge it. Therefore, including a radiologist evaluation of synthetic images significantly improves the reliability of the image quality evaluation. 5. Validity of our method by using synthesized images produced from our SGVI method to pre-train in the self-supervised mode the Swin-UNETR model of a particular data-set of images originating from a certain hospital, yielding the most accurate and competitive results when the pre-trained model is used on another data-set from a different hospital and that significantly outperforms the non-pre-trained version, primarily for low-data settings. A setting highly relevant in medical imaging.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
